crossRefs.label: Disconnect the tie rod end from the knuckle using the special tool.

blocks.text: Disconnect the brake hose (A) from the brake hose bracket (B). Remove the caliper (C) from the knuckle and secure the caliper with a wire or similar.

items: When securing the caliper, be very careful not to damage other parts.
Do not secure the caliper with brake hose.
Do not forcefully twist the brake hose.

items.text: When securing the caliper, be very careful not to damage other parts.
When securing the caliper, be very careful not to damage other parts.
Do not secure the caliper with brake hose.
Do not secure the caliper with brake hose.
Do not forcefully twist the brake hose.
Do not forcefully twist the brake hose.

blocks.text: Do not disconnect the wheel sensor cord.
Caulk (B) the spindle nut (A) and remove the nut. Replace the spindle nut with a new one at the time of installation.

Remove the split pin (B) from the ball joint connection of the tie rod end (A) and remove the nut (C). When installing, replace the split pin with a new one and bend it as shown in the figure. Disconnect the tie rod end from the knuckle using the special tool.

refs.label: Disconnect the tie rod end from the knuckle using the special tool.

blocks.text: 8. Stabilizer linkage disconnection (for vehicles equipped with stabilizer)
Disconnect the damper (A) from the stabilizer link (B).

items.text: Hold the joint pin (C) with the hex wrench (D) and loosen the flange nut (E).
Hold the joint pin (C) with the hex wrench (D) and loosen the flange nut (E).
When installing, replace the flange nut with a new one.
When installing, replace the flange nut with a new one.

blocks.text: Remove the clip (B) from the ball joint connection on the lower arm (A) and remove the grooved nut (C). At the time of installation, replace the grooved nut with a new one. Install the special tool and disconnect the knuckle from the lower arm. To remove the left side ball joint, rotate the knuckle to the right, and to remove the right side ball joint, rotate the knuckle to the left and install the special tool from the rear.

refs.label: Install the special tool and disconnect the knuckle from the lower arm.

blocks.text: Disconnect the damper (A) from the knuckle (B) and pull out the drive shaft (C) from the knuckle. At the time of installation, replace the mounting bolts and nuts of the damper and knuckle with new ones.

items: Be careful not to pull the knuckle outward too hard, as this will dislodge the roller of the driveshaft inboard joint. Remove the outboard joint from the knuckle by lightly pulling the knuckle and striking the end of the drive shaft (D) with a plastic hammer.
Molybdenum grease shall be applied to the contact surface (E) of the outboard joint and wheel bearing during installation.

items.text: Be careful not to pull the knuckle outward too hard, as this will dislodge the roller of the driveshaft inboard joint. Remove the outboard joint from the knuckle by lightly pulling the knuckle and striking the end of the drive shaft (D) with a plastic hammer.

Be careful not to pull the knuckle outward too hard, as this will dislodge the roller of the driveshaft inboard joint. Remove the outboard joint from the knuckle by lightly pulling the knuckle and striking the end of the drive shaft (D) with a plastic hammer.
Molybdenum grease shall be applied to the contact surface (E) of the outboard joint and wheel bearing during installation.
Molybdenum grease shall be applied to the contact surface (E) of the outboard joint and wheel bearing during installation.

blocks.text: Pay attention to the following items and install in the reverse order of removal.

items.text: For tightening the bushings, tighten the bushings to the specified torque with the vehicle weight applied after mounting the bushings in a temporarily tightened condition. Do not apply a jack to the ball joint of the lower arm.

For tightening the bushings, tighten the bushings to the specified torque with the vehicle weight applied after mounting the bushings in a temporarily tightened condition. Do not apply a jack to the ball joint of the lower arm.
Ball joint boots must be checked for dents or other deformities, and if deformed, they must be corrected before installation. If the boots are installed with deformation, they may be damaged.
Ball joint boots must be checked for dents or other deformities, and if deformed, they must be corrected before installation. If the boots are installed with deformation, they may be damaged.
Ball joint connections (ball pin taper, thread, nut seat) shall be degreased, cleaned, and free of grease.
Ball joint connections (ball pin taper, thread, nut seat) shall be degreased, cleaned, and free of grease.
When tightening the grooved nut, first tighten at the lower torque limit, and then tighten until the grooved nut and joint pin holes align. Do not back-tighten in the loosening direction.
When tightening the grooved nut, first tighten at the lower torque limit, and then tighten until the grooved nut and joint pin holes align. Do not back-tighten in the loosening direction.
Apply a small amount of engine oil to the nut seat surface before installation, and caulk the spindle nut after tightening to the specified torque.
Apply a small amount of engine oil to the nut seat surface before installation, and caulk the spindle nut after tightening to the specified torque.

blocks.text: Remove the hub (B) from the knuckle (A) using the special tool and press machine.

items: Knuckles shall be held in place with a press attachment (C) or similar.
The attachment should be applied in a position that does not deform the splash guard (D).

items.text: Knuckles shall be held in place with a press attachment (C) or similar.
Knuckles shall be held in place with a press attachment (C) or similar.
The attachment should be applied in a position that does not deform the splash guard (D).
The attachment should be applied in a position that does not deform the splash guard (D).

blocks.text: Remove the bearing inner race (C) from the hub (B) using a special tool, a press, or a commercially available bearing race puller set (A).
Remove the circlip (A) and splash guard (B) from the knuckle (C).
Remove the wheel bearing (B) from the knuckle (A) using the special tool and press machine. Do not reuse removed wheel bearings.
Place the removed wheel bearing (A) on top of the new wheel bearing (B) and press-fit the bearing into the knuckle (D) using a steel plate (C), special tool and press machine.

items: The inner surface of the wheel bearing press fitting on the knuckle must be cleaned before assembly.
Note the direction of the wheel bearing press-fit. Press-fit the wheel bearing with the encoder (brown) mounted with the inside seal (E) side toward the knuckle.
The encoder surface should be free of grease, dust, and dirt.
Keep magnetized tools and other objects away from the encoder surface.
Do not damage the encoder surface during press fitting.

items.text: The inner surface of the wheel bearing press fitting on the knuckle must be cleaned before assembly.
The inner surface of the wheel bearing press fitting on the knuckle must be cleaned before assembly.
Note the direction of the wheel bearing press-fit. Press-fit the wheel bearing with the encoder (brown) mounted with the inside seal (E) side toward the knuckle.
Note the direction of the wheel bearing press-fit. Press-fit the wheel bearing with the encoder (brown) mounted with the inside seal (E) side toward the knuckle.
The encoder surface should be free of grease, dust, and dirt.
The encoder surface should be free of grease, dust, and dirt.
Keep magnetized tools and other objects away from the encoder surface.
Keep magnetized tools and other objects away from the encoder surface.
Do not damage the encoder surface during press fitting.
Do not damage the encoder surface during press fitting.

blocks.text: Attach the circlip (A) and splash guard (B) to the knuckle (C).
Press-fit the hub (A) into the wheel bearing (B) using the special tool and press machine.

items: The surfaces of the hub press fitting shall be cleaned prior to assembly.
Keep magnetized tools and other objects away from the encoder surface.
Do not damage the encoder surface during press fitting.
After assembly, make sure that the encoder surface is free of oil, grease, dust, and dirt.

items.text: The surfaces of the hub press fitting shall be cleaned prior to assembly.

Keep magnetized tools and other objects away from the encoder surface.

Do not damage the encoder surface during press fitting.

After assembly, make sure that the encoder surface is free of oil, grease, dust, and dirt.
